data.title: ATI (ATI) Net Margin Of 8.8% Tests Bullish Profit Expansion Narrative
data.description:
ATI (ATI) has opened Q1 2026 earnings season with Q4 2025 revenue of US$1.2 billion and basic EPS of US$0.71, setting the stage against a current share price of US$155.46 as investors focus on how these results fit into the company’s recent earnings trajectory. Over the past year, quarterly revenue has ranged from US$1.1 billion to US$1.2 billion, while basic EPS has moved between US$0.64 and US$0.80. This provides a clear view of how the top and bottom line have tracked together across recent reporting periods. With trailing twelve month basic EPS at US$2.92 and net margin at 8.8%, attention now turns to whether ATI’s profitability profile is giving investors enough comfort around the sustainability of these earnings.

See our full analysis for ATI.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how ATI’s latest earnings line up with the key market narratives on growth, margins, and risk that have developed over the past few years.

See what the community is saying about ATI

Advertisement

Net margin at 8.8% with four quarter EPS above US$0.68

  • Across the last four reported quarters, ATI’s basic EPS sat between US$0.68 and US$0.80, while trailing net margin was 8.8% compared with 8.4% a year earlier, on trailing twelve month net income of US$404.3 million from US$4.6b in revenue.
  • Consensus narrative expects higher margin businesses to matter more over time, and the current 8.8% net margin gives a concrete starting point to test that view.
    • Analysts in the balanced view are assuming net margin can move from 8.8% to 13.5%, supported by contracts that tie pricing to inflation and focus on higher value alloys.
    • The fact that recent quarterly net income has been in the US$96.6 million to US$110 million range shows the earnings base that needs to expand for that margin step up to play out.

Earnings growth slowing versus 5 year pace

  • ATI’s 1 year earnings growth of 9.9% sits well below its 5 year earnings growth rate of 65.1% per year, even though trailing twelve month EPS is US$2.92 on US$404.3 million of net income.
  • Bulls argue the slower recent growth is a pause before another leg up in profits, but the numbers set a high bar for that optimism.
    • The bullish narrative looks for earnings to reach US$861.9 million, which is more than double the current US$404.3 million level, and assumes revenue grows 9.4% a year, above the consensus 7.7% forecast.
    • At the same time, bullish analysts expect margins to rise from 8.8% to 14.4%, so the current 9.9% earnings growth rate and single digit margin leave a lot of progress to be made to match that path.
Have a look at how the optimistic case stacks up against the actual earnings path so far: 🐂 ATI Bull Case.

Premium 52.5x P/E with DCF fair value below price

  • ATI is trading on a trailing P/E of 52.5x at a share price of US$155.46, compared with a peer average of 41.3x and an Aerospace & Defense industry average of 35.5x, while the provided DCF fair value is US$140.59.
  • Bears focus on this valuation gap, and the current earnings profile gives some support to their caution.
    • The bearish narrative assumes earnings can climb from around US$398.7 million to US$612.9 million, yet the share price already sits above the DCF fair value of US$140.59, which leaves less room if growth follows the lower 4.3% revenue path they outline.
    • With earnings forecast to grow 16.3% a year from the current US$2.92 EPS base and revenue forecast at 7.7% a year, skeptics question whether that growth and an 8.8% margin are enough to support a 52.5x P/E against cheaper peers.
If you want to see why some investors question paying a premium multiple here, check the cautious case next: 🐻 ATI Bear Case.

data.title: Viavi Solutions (VIAV) Q3 Profit Challenges Bearish Narratives On Persistent Losses
data.description:
Viavi Solutions (VIAV) just posted Q3 2026 results with revenue of US$406.8 million and basic EPS of US$0.03, while on a trailing twelve month basis revenue stands at US$1.4 billion with a basic EPS loss of US$0.24. Over recent quarters, the company has seen revenue move from US$270.8 million in Q2 2025 to US$406.8 million in Q3 2026, alongside basic EPS shifting from US$0.04 in Q2 2025 to US$0.03 in the latest quarter and swinging between profits and losses in the periods in between. This puts the spotlight firmly on how quickly margins can stabilise from here.

See our full analysis for Viavi Solutions.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings print lines up with the most common narratives around Viavi Solutions, highlighting where the data supports the story and where it pushes back.

See what the community is saying about Viavi Solutions

Advertisement

Revenue climbs toward US$1.4b run-rate

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, Viavi has generated US$1.4b of revenue, up from US$1.0b a year earlier, with quarterly revenue moving from US$270.8 million in Q2 2025 to US$406.8 million in Q3 2026.
  • Supporters of the bullish narrative point to this revenue ramp as backing long term data center and fiber upgrade themes. However, the trend also has to carry the weight of current unprofitability:
    • Bulls highlight forecasts that revenue could grow around 18.3% per year and that structural demand for high speed optical test equipment and aerospace and defense solutions can sustain that trajectory.
    • Against that, trailing twelve month net income is still a loss of US$55.1 million and EPS over the same period is a loss of US$0.24, so the stronger top line has not yet translated into consistent profits.
Supporters who want to see how the optimistic case lines up with detailed forecasts and segment drivers can go deeper into the bull argument in 🐂 Viavi Solutions Bull Case.

Profit swing: Q3 profit, TTM still loss making

  • Q3 2026 delivered net income of US$6.4 million and basic EPS of roughly US$0.03, yet over the last twelve months the company still recorded a net loss of US$55.1 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.24.
  • Skeptics in the bearish camp focus on this profit gap when they argue that near term earnings risk remains high, even with growth stories in place:
    • Bears point out that the company has been unprofitable on a trailing basis and that losses have grown at about 51.3% per year over the past five years, which they see as a warning against leaning too hard on a single profitable quarter.
    • They also flag that interest coverage is weak, with earnings not comfortably covering interest expense. The path from a small Q3 profit to sustained, debt friendly earnings is therefore a key point of pressure for the cautious view.
Skeptical readers who want to see how these risks are framed around future scenarios can review the detailed cautious case in 🐻 Viavi Solutions Bear Case.

High P/S multiple vs peers and DCF fair value

  • At the current share price of US$52.40 and a trailing twelve month revenue base of US$1.4b, Viavi is trading on a P/S of 8.9x, compared with 2.6x for the broader US communications industry and slightly below the peer group at 9.2x, while the DCF fair value provided is US$39.20.
  • Analysts who take a more balanced, consensus style view see this mix of growth and valuation as a trade off rather than a clear bargain, and they compare it with their standardised target price:
    • The consensus narrative referenced in the data expects revenue and earnings to improve meaningfully over the next few years, which helps explain why the stock trades at a higher P/S multiple than the industry average.
    • At the same time, the DCF fair value of US$39.20 is below the current market price and the standardised analyst target of US$64.43 sits above it, so investors are effectively weighing a rich current multiple, stronger growth forecasts and a mixed valuation picture before deciding how comfortable they are with the risk profile.

data.title: Is NIQ Global Intelligence (NIQ) Offering An Opportunity After This Year’s 30% Share Price Slide
data.description:
• This article considers whether NIQ Global Intelligence, at around US$10.93, offers value or simply appears cheap on the surface by examining what the current price might really imply.
  • The share price has eased back recently, with a 7‑day return of a 2.6% decline, a 30‑day return of a 3.9% decline, and a year‑to‑date return of a 30.7% decline.
  • Recent coverage has focused on the stock's weaker near‑term returns and what they might signal about investor expectations, which helps explain why sentiment has cooled. At the same time, valuation tools have started to flag NIQ Global Intelligence as potentially pricing in a lot of caution.
  • On Simply Wall St's valuation checks, NIQ Global Intelligence scores a full 6 out of 6. The next sections break down how different valuation approaches view the stock and introduce a potentially more useful way to think about value at the end of the article.

NIQ Global Intelligence delivered 0.0% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Media industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: NIQ Global Intelligence Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to today to get a single present value per share.

For NIQ Global Intelligence, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $9.86 million. Analysts provide forecasts out to 2028, with free cash flow for that year projected at $442.00 million. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extrapolates cash flows out to 2035, with discounted values provided for each year to build the total equity value.

On this basis, the DCF model suggests an estimated intrinsic value of about $33.87 per share, compared with the current share price of around $10.93. That gap implies the shares are trading at a 67.7% discount to the DCF estimate, which points to a materially lower market price than the modelled cash flow value.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests NIQ Global Intelligence is undervalued by 67.7%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 51 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for NIQ Global Intelligence.

Approach 2: NIQ Global Intelligence Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings are not yet a steady guide, the price to sales, or P/S, ratio can be a useful way to think about value because it compares the share price to the revenue the business generates rather than to current profits.

In general, higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can justify a higher normal or fair P/S multiple, while slower expected growth and higher risk usually call for a lower multiple. So, context really matters when you compare any single P/S number.

NIQ Global Intelligence currently trades on a P/S ratio of 0.77x. This sits below the Media industry average P/S of 1.08x and also below the peer group average of 1.86x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for NIQ Global Intelligence is 1.28x, which is a proprietary estimate of what the P/S might be given factors such as the company’s earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics.

Compared with simple peer or industry averages, the Fair Ratio aims to be more tailored because it adjusts for company specific growth, risks and profitability, not just broad sector comparisons. Set against this 1.28x Fair Ratio, the current 0.77x P/S indicates that the shares are pricing in more caution than the model implies.

Result: UNDERVALUED

data.title: How Moderna (MRNA) Is Recasting Its Vaccine Platform After EU Approval of Its First Combo Shot
data.description:
• In April 2026, the European Commission granted marketing authorization for Moderna’s mCOMBRIAX (mRNA-1083), the first approved mRNA combination influenza and COVID-19 vaccine for adults aged 50 and over across the EU, Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Norway.
  • Around the same time, the Global Virus Network publicly backed Moderna’s progress on its mRNA H5N1 avian influenza vaccine, underscoring how the company’s mRNA platform is being applied to multiple high-impact respiratory threats beyond COVID-19.
  • We’ll now examine how the EU approval of mCOMBRIAX could influence Moderna’s investment narrative and expectations for its expanding mRNA pipeline.

To own Moderna today, you have to believe its mRNA platform can move beyond a single COVID-19 product into a broader, commercially viable vaccine portfolio. The EU approval of mCOMBRIAX adds a new respiratory product and may help soften near term revenue volatility, but it does not remove the core risk that seasonal demand and competition could still leave overall sales and earnings uneven for some time.

The Global Virus Network’s backing of Moderna’s mRNA H5N1 avian influenza vaccine effort is especially relevant here, because it highlights how the same platform behind mCOMBRIAX is being applied to another high profile respiratory threat. Together, these updates keep respiratory vaccines at the center of Moderna’s catalyst story, even as the company contends with ongoing losses and a business still heavily tied to vaccination trends.

Approach 1: Amazon.com Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of the cash a company could generate in the future and discounts those cash flows back to what they might be worth in today's dollars. It is essentially asking what a stream of future cash flows could reasonably be worth now.

For Amazon.com, the model used here is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $37.1b. Analysts contribute Free Cash Flow estimates for the next few years, and Simply Wall St extrapolates these further out. On that basis, projected Free Cash Flow for 2030 is $208.5b, with a series of annual projections between now and 2035, all expressed in $ and discounted back to today.

When those discounted cash flows are added up, the resulting estimated intrinsic value is $450.28 per share. Compared with the current share price of $265.06, the model implies an intrinsic discount of 41.1%, so the shares screen as undervalued on this DCF view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Amazon.com Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand for how much investors are currently paying for each dollar of earnings. It is simple to read, and it ties the share price directly to the bottom line, which is usually what drives long term returns.

What counts as a “normal” P/E partly depends on how quickly earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings appear. Higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk usually support a higher P/E, while weaker growth and higher risk tend to be reflected in a lower multiple.

Amazon.com currently trades on a P/E of 31.4x. That is above the Multiline Retail industry average P/E of 19.5x and above the peer group average of 27.5x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” for Amazon.com of 38.6x, which is the P/E that would be expected given factors such as its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market value and specific risks.

This Fair Ratio is more tailored than simple peer or industry comparisons because it adjusts for company specific characteristics rather than assuming all retailers deserve similar multiples. Comparing the current P/E of 31.4x with the Fair Ratio of 38.6x points to Amazon.com trading below that modeled level.

Result: UNDERVALUED

data.description:
Kansai Electric Power Company (TSE:9503) has wrapped up FY 2026 with fourth quarter revenue of ¥1.1 trillion and basic EPS of ¥35.77, as investors weigh these figures against the current share price of ¥2,401.5. Over recent periods, the company has seen quarterly revenue range from ¥917.8 billion to ¥1.1 trillion in FY 2026, while basic EPS moved between ¥35.77 and ¥120.07. In this context, margins and overall profitability remain key areas of focus as you assess how durable these results may be.

TTM profit margin at 9.4% with softer recent quarters

  • Trailing twelve month net income sits at ¥380,051 million on ¥4.1b of revenue, which works out to a 9.4% net margin compared with 9.7% a year earlier.
  • For a bearish view, one notable point is that within that 9.4% margin, quarterly net income in FY 2026 moved between ¥39,852 million and ¥133,768 million, which lines up with the concern that earnings are forecast to decline by about 2.7% per year over the next three years rather than extend the strong five year growth trend.
    • Critics point to TTM net income easing from ¥420,364 million in FY 2025 Q4 to ¥380,051 million in FY 2026 Q4 as one sign that the earlier growth phase is not repeating in the latest period.
    • The modest revenue forecast of about 0.6% per year, compared with a 5.9% forecast for the broader JP market, also fits with a cautious stance on how much profit growth this margin level can support.

P/E of 7x versus DCF fair value gap

  • At a share price of ¥2,401.5 the stock trades on a P/E of 7x, below the 14.4x JP market and the 17.3x Asian electric utilities average, while a DCF fair value of ¥1,361.60 sits below the current price.
  • Supporters of a bullish angle often highlight the low P/E relative to market and sector. However, the comparison with the DCF fair value and the flagged debt coverage risk gives a mixed picture that investors need to weigh.
    • The P/E discount versus the broader JP market and sector suggests the shares are priced more cheaply than many peers, yet the modelled DCF fair value level of ¥1,361.60 is well under the ¥2,401.5 trading price.
    • On top of that, the assessment that operating cash flow does not comfortably cover debt adds another hard constraint for anyone arguing that the valuation alone makes the stock look straightforwardly cheap.

Curious how this mix of modest valuation multiples, DCF gap, and earnings trends fits into the bigger investor story for Kansai Electric, and how other investors are framing it in their own words, Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Strong five year EPS growth versus softer TTM trend

  • Over the last five years earnings grew at about 37% per year, yet TTM EPS has eased from ¥498.75 at FY 2025 Q3 to ¥341.14 at FY 2026 Q4, while quarterly EPS in FY 2026 ranged from ¥35.77 to ¥120.07.
  • What challenges a straightforward bullish narrative is this contrast between the strong historical growth rate and the more recent TTM profile that includes a lower EPS in the latest quarter, plus forecasts that point to earnings declining by about 2.7% per year over the next three years.
    • Bulls may emphasise the five year growth figure as evidence of a solid track record, but the step down in TTM EPS from ¥498.75 to ¥341.14 shows that the recent window does not match that earlier pace.
    • When that TTM pattern is paired with the modest revenue growth forecast and slightly lower net margin, it becomes clear that the backward looking growth number and the more cautious forward view tell two different stories.
